---
title: "Combinatorial surface-marker targets for prostate cancer"
subtitle: "Patient-robust discovery of AND / NOT dual-antigen pairs, benchmarked against PSMA-PSCA"
author: "William Guesdon &nbsp;·&nbsp; Built with Claude: Life Sciences &nbsp;·&nbsp; Research Track 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme_dark.scss]
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    slide-number: false
    embed-resources: true
    center: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
---

## The project

**Antigen-directed therapies struggle to hit the tumor without hitting healthy tissue.** Few surface
antigens are truly absent from every normal cell type, so single-target CAR-T, T-cell engagers and ADCs
carry on-target off-tumor toxicity.

[Logic-gated targeting]{.accent} engages a cell only when two conditions hold:

- an **AND gate** &nbsp;— both markers present on the tumor
- a **NOT gate** &nbsp;— an activator on, unless a blocker marks a healthy cell to spare

::: {.muted}
**The question:** which pair of cell-surface proteins marks prostate cancer cells while sparing every
healthy human cell type? We score marker pairs **per patient** in single-cell data, benchmarked against
the known **PSMA-PSCA** pair.
:::

::: {.notes}
About a minute. Most targeted therapies for solid tumors fail on safety, not potency: a single antigen is
almost never absent from every healthy tissue. Logic-gated targeting fixes this by requiring two
conditions before a cell is engaged. We ask, for prostate cancer, which pair of surface proteins marks
the tumor while sparing every healthy cell type. The key discipline is that we score every pair per
patient, never pooling cells, and we benchmark against PSMA-PSCA, a pair we already know works.
:::

## Method and results

::: {.muted}
Per-patient scan, never pooled &nbsp;·&nbsp; matched benign-prostate control &nbsp;·&nbsp; assay-matched,
donor-robust healthy denominator &nbsp;·&nbsp; a positive-control gate: recover PSMA-PSCA before nominating.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="stats">
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">~6&times;</div><div class="lab">PSMA-PSCA safety collapse recovered (0.9 &rarr; 0.15)</div></div>
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">0.69</div><div class="lab">PSMA &times; STEAP1 per-patient coverage &middot; Pareto-optimal</div></div>
  <div class="stat"><div class="num">369k</div><div class="lab">cells, 6 studies &middot; independent replication</div></div>
</div>
```

::: {.fragment .res}
[✓]{.k} **The method re-finds the known answer.** PSMA alone is broadly off-tumor (0.85 in duodenum), PSCA
alone (0.95 in bladder); requiring **both** collapses the worst liability to **0.15**.
:::

::: {.fragment .res}
[→]{.k} **Nominated lead: PSMA x STEAP1** &nbsp;— Pareto-optimal among clean pairs, both antigens have
clinical-grade binders. Not a novel pair (a dual PSMA/STEAP1 ADC is already in phase 1); the contribution
is the AND framing and the controls.
:::

::: {.fragment .res}
[⚠]{.k} **An honest limit, caught by an independent cohort.** In HuPSA, tumor-specificity replicates, but
coverage is **lost in metastatic and neuroendocrine disease** — the population most therapies target.
:::

::: {.notes}
About a minute. The method: score every pair per patient, keep a matched benign-prostate control from the
same tumors, and compare against a healthy atlas restricted to the same assay and made robust across
donors. A gate: we must recover PSMA-PSCA first. First result, our trust signal: each antigen alone is
broadly expressed off the prostate, but requiring both collapses the worst liability roughly sixfold. The
method re-finds the known answer. Second, it nominates PSMA times STEAP1, which is Pareto-optimal among
the clean pairs and has clinical binders; we are careful to say the pair is not novel, an ADC already
exists, our contribution is the rigorous AND framing. Third, and this is what good science looks like: an
independent 369-thousand-cell cohort confirms tumor specificity but shows the coverage is lost in
advanced disease. The external cohort caught a real limitation.
:::

## Live demo {background-color="#0A0F29"}

::: {.demo-title}
Website &nbsp;+&nbsp; web application
:::

::: {.tagline}
The reproducible analysis, the report, and an explorer for the marker pairs.
:::

::: {.demo-cue}
▶ &nbsp; switching to a live demo
:::

::: {.notes}
About a minute, live. Switch to the website and the web application here: walk through the reproducible
report and the interactive explorer for the candidate pairs. Close on the one-line takeaway: a
disciplined, benchmark-anchored method that produces an honest, hypothesis-generating finding, not an
overstated claim.
:::
